The overall road quality, the specific style of the concrete utility poles, and the right-hand traffic are all perfectly consistent with Iranian residential streets.

Upon closer inspection of the architectural details and the specific application of the blue-and-white curb paint, the evidence aligns more strongly with Iran. The combination of the flat-roofed masonry construction, the specific style of the metal window grates (common in Iranian residential areas), and the ubiquitous use of blue/white curb markings for parking regulation in Iranian cities outweighs the similar patterns found in Armenia.
